fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Nevada Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Nevada - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity nevada, how to transfer audiologist license to nevada, audiologist compact nevada, nevada audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Examiners for Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology
board.parent_dept: Nevada Board of Examiners for Audiology and SLP
board.mailing_address: 1755 E. Plumb Lane, Suite 237, Reno, NV 89502
board.physical_address: 5011 Meadowood Mall Way, Suite 300, Reno, NV 89502

quick_facts.exam_name: Audiology Examination of the Praxis Series

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in Nevada.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for your official graduate transcripts, verifying the required degree, to be sent directly from your educational institution to the Board.

steps.title: Submit Praxis Exam Scores
steps.description: Have your official Praxis Audiology (Code 5343) exam scores sent directly from ETS to the Board.

steps.title: Complete Jurisprudence Exam
steps.description: Download, complete, and submit the Nevada Audiology/Speech-Language Pathology Jurisprudence Exam, available on the Board's website under 'Forms,' along with your application.

steps.title: Complete Fingerprint Background Check
steps.description: Undergo a fingerprint background check through the Nevada Department of Public Safety. Follow the specific instructions provided within the online application.

steps.title: Submit Clinical Fellowship Verification (If Applicable)
steps.description: If applying for an initial or provisional license requiring supervised experience, ensure your Verification of Clinical Fellowship (VCF) form is submitted to the Board.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Nevada audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Nevada have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers audiologist practice in Nevada. To work in Nevada, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Nevada?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Nevada while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in Nevada
hero.dek: To apply for an Audiologist license in Nevada, register and submit your application through the Nevada Board of Examiners for Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology Online Portal.

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.portal_name: Nevada Board of Examiners for Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology Online Portal
official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the Nevada Board of Examiners for Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology Online Portal, click 'Register' at the top right, and follow the prompts to create your account. Once registered, log in to start a new application.
